title: Environment Considerations for DB2
text: Environment Considerations for DB2 When using the DB2 connector, you need to be aware of some limitations that the platform and operating environment impose. Network Considerations Both the Uniface Server and DB2 network capabilities can be used. The Uniface Server is used to connect Uniface to the connector, and the DB2 network capabilities are used to communicate between the connector and the DB2 database. System Parameters On Microsoft Windows, DB2 sets the LOCKTIMEOUT database parameter to infinite by default, meaning that when a deadlock situation occurs, Uniface will hang indefinitely, instead of returning status code -11, as on other platforms or other databases. To avoid this situation, the LOCKTIMEOUT parameter should be given a realistic value, for example, 30 seconds. On RS/6000, setting the shell variable LC_NUMERIC affects the connector behavior. The DB2 connector on RS/6000 requires the environmental variable LC_NUMERIC , which must be set as follows to ensure correct numeric formatting: LC_NUMERIC = en_US Copy CAUTION: The environment variable LC_NUMERIC must be set to a valid value on RS/6000 systems. If not set, it can cause the operating system to become unstable. Using DB2 for the Repository You can use DB2 to store Uniface system information. However, this can result in delays whenever the connector encounters locked records, because the connector waits until the lock is released. When using DB2 as the Repository, ensure that database has a 32 KB-page size. For an existing database, add a 32 KB-page tablespace with a 32 KB-page buffer pool. You should check the maximum number of open cursors allowed on your system, because this can diminish performance if you use it to store Uniface system information. Was this topic helpful?
